findings.evidence: lines 80-107 define and prove general map_id/map_comp at Bialgebra level; lines 192-200 and 249-253 repeat them inside BaseChange with ids hard-coded (grep 'map_comp (g : B →ₐc[k] C)' and 'map_id :')
findings.fix: delete the local map_comp (and map_id) and obtain them by `rw [Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_comp]` or similar; same for map_antipode
findings.issue: Bialgebra.TensorProduct.map_comp proves the four-map case; HopfAlgebra.BaseChange.map_comp immediately re-proves the two-id special case instead of deriving it.

findings.evidence: grep 'private lemma antipode_comp' and 'private lemma map_antipode'; they are used only by the HopfMap section 240
findings.fix: move the antipode preservation to after the Hopf section or obtain from the general antipode_comp once the general linear-map form lives at HopfAlgHom level
findings.issue: BialgHom.antipode_comp and map_antipode assume full [HopfAlgebra k A] [HopfAlgebra k B] to prove a property that only needs the bialgebra map + Hopf antipode lemma; placed before the general tensor case.

summary: Special-case bialgebra and Hopf lemmas duplicate the general functoriality and antipode results proved earlier in the same file.
